quiz.title: Unit 7 Quiz 6, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Advanced Stoichiometry and Biochemical Applications
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Mass-Mass Stoichiometry, Mole-Mass Calculations, Biochemical Applications, Percent Composition, Molecular Mass Calculations

questions.question:
What mass of iron is produced from 159.7 g of iron(III) oxide using carbon reduction?

$\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3 + 3\text{C} \rightarrow 2\text{Fe} + 3\text{CO}$
questions.explanation:
Molar mass Fe₂O₃ = (2 × 55.85) + (3 × 16.00) = 111.70 + 48.00 = 159.70 g/mol. Moles Fe₂O₃ = 159.7 g / 159.70 g/mol = 1.00 mol.

Mole ratio Fe₂O₃:Fe = 1:2. Moles Fe = 2.00 mol.

Molar mass Fe = 55.85 g/mol. Mass Fe = 2.00 mol × 55.85 g/mol = 111.7 g.

questions.question:
How many grams of hydrogen gas are needed to produce 68.0 g of ammonia?

$\text{N}_2 + 3\text{H}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{NH}_3$
questions.explanation:
Molar mass NH₃ = 14.01 + (3 × 1.008) = 17.03 g/mol. Moles NH₃ = 68.0 g / 17.03 g/mol = 3.99 mol ≈ 4.00 mol.

Mole ratio NH₃:H₂ = 2:3. Moles H₂ = 4.00 mol NH₃ × (3 mol H₂ / 2 mol NH₃) = 6.00 mol H₂.

Molar mass H₂ = 2.016 g/mol. Mass H₂ = 6.00 mol × 2.016 g/mol = 12.1 g ≈ 12.0 g.

questions.question: A sample of testosterone ($\text{C}_{19}\text{H}_{28}\text{O}_2$) is analyzed and found to contain $7.00 \times 10^{20}$ atoms of hydrogen. How many atoms of carbon are present in the same sample?
questions.explanation:
In one molecule of testosterone, the ratio of hydrogen atoms to carbon atoms is 28:19.

Atoms of C = atoms of H × (19/28)
= $7.00 \times 10^{20} \times \frac{19}{28}$
= $7.00 \times 10^{20} \times 0.6786$
= $4.75 \times 10^{20}$ carbon atoms.

questions.question: A sample of testosterone ($\text{C}_{19}\text{H}_{28}\text{O}_2$) is analyzed and found to contain $7.00 \times 10^{20}$ atoms of hydrogen. How many molecules of testosterone are in the sample?
questions.explanation:
Each molecule of testosterone contains 28 hydrogen atoms.

Number of molecules = total hydrogen atoms ÷ hydrogen atoms per molecule
= $7.00 \times 10^{20} \div 28$
= $0.25 \times 10^{20}$
= $2.50 \times 10^{19}$ molecules.

Therefore, the sample contains $2.50 \times 10^{19}$ molecules of testosterone.

questions.question: A sample of testosterone ($\text{C}_{19}\text{H}_{28}\text{O}_2$) contains $7.00 \times 10^{20}$ atoms of hydrogen. How many moles of testosterone does this represent? (Avogadro's number = $6.022 \times 10^{23}$)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Find molecules of testosterone.
Each molecule contains 28 H atoms.
Molecules = $(7.00 \times 10^{20} \text{ atoms H}) \div (28 \text{ atoms H/molecule}) = 2.50 \times 10^{19}$ molecules.

Step 2: Convert molecules to moles.
Moles = molecules $\div$ Avogadro's number
= $(2.50 \times 10^{19}) \div (6.022 \times 10^{23})$
= $4.15 \times 10^{-5}$ mol.

Therefore, the sample contains $4.15 \times 10^{-5}$ moles of testosterone.

questions.question: A sample of testosterone ($\text{C}_{19}\text{H}_{28}\text{O}_2$) contains $7.00 \times 10^{20}$ atoms of hydrogen. What is the total mass of this sample? (Avogadro's number = $6.022 \times 10^{23}$)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Find molecules of testosterone.
Each molecule contains 28 H atoms.
Molecules = $(7.00 \times 10^{20} \text{ atoms H}) \div (28 \text{ atoms H/molecule}) = 2.50 \times 10^{19}$ molecules.

Step 2: Convert molecules to moles.
Moles = molecules $\div$ Avogadro's number
= $(2.50 \times 10^{19}) \div (6.022 \times 10^{23})$
= $4.15 \times 10^{-5}$ mol.

Step 3: Calculate molar mass of testosterone.
C: $19 \times 12.01 = 228.19$ g/mol
H: $28 \times 1.008 = 28.224$ g/mol
O: $2 \times 16.00 = 32.00$ g/mol
Total molar mass = $228.19 + 28.224 + 32.00 = 288.41$ g/mol

Step 4: Calculate mass of sample.
Mass = moles $\times$ molar mass
= $(4.15 \times 10^{-5} \text{ mol}) \times (288.41 \text{ g/mol})$
= $0.01197$ g $\approx 0.0120$ g.

Therefore, the sample has a mass of $0.0120$ g.

questions.question: A compound contains 40.0% carbon, 6.7% hydrogen, and 53.3% oxygen by mass. What is its empirical formula?
questions.explanation:
Assume 100 g sample: 
C: 40.0 g ÷ 12.01 g/mol = 3.33 mol 
H: 6.7 g ÷ 1.008 g/mol = 6.65 mol 
O: 53.3 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 3.33 mol 

Divide by smallest (3.33): 
C: 3.33 ÷ 3.33 = 1 
H: 6.65 ÷ 3.33 = 2 
O: 3.33 ÷ 3.33 = 1 

Empirical formula = CH₂O

questions.question: What is the empirical formula of a compound with 43.6% phosphorus and 56.4% oxygen?
questions.explanation:
Assume 100 g sample:
P: 43.6 g ÷ 30.97 g/mol = 1.408 mol
O: 56.4 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 3.525 mol

Divide by smallest (1.408):
P: 1.408 ÷ 1.408 = 1
O: 3.525 ÷ 1.408 = 2.5

Multiply by 2 to get whole numbers:
P: 1 × 2 = 2
O: 2.5 × 2 = 5

Empirical formula = P₂O₅

questions.question: A compound contains 72.4% iron and 27.6% oxygen. What is its empirical formula?
questions.explanation:
Assume 100 g sample:
Fe: 72.4 g ÷ 55.85 g/mol = 1.296 mol
O: 27.6 g ÷ 16.00 g/mol = 1.725 mol

Divide by smallest (1.296):
Fe: 1.296 ÷ 1.296 = 1
O: 1.725 ÷ 1.296 = 1.33

Multiply by 3 to get whole numbers:
Fe: 1 × 3 = 3
O: 1.33 × 3 = 4

Empirical formula = Fe₃O₄

Fe₃O₄ (magnetite) contains 72.4% iron, which matches the given data. Fe₂O₃ (hematite) would be 69.9% iron, and FeO would be 77.8% iron.

questions.question:
What mass of sulfuric acid is needed to produce 36.0 g of water in neutralization with excess sodium hydroxide?

$\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 + 2\text{NaOH} \rightarrow \text{Na}_2\text{SO}_4 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$
questions.explanation:
Molar mass H₂O = 18.02 g/mol. Moles H₂O = 36.0 g / 18.02 g/mol = 2.00 mol.

Mole ratio H₂O:H₂SO₄ = 2:1. Moles H₂SO₄ = 2.00 mol H₂O × (1 mol H₂SO₄ / 2 mol H₂O) = 1.00 mol.

Molar mass H₂SO₄ = (2 × 1.008) + 32.06 + (4 × 16.00) = 2.016 + 32.06 + 64.00 = 98.08 g/mol. Mass H₂SO₄ = 1.00 mol × 98.08 g/mol = 98.1 g ≈ 98.0 g.

questions.question:
How many grams of potassium chlorate must decompose to produce 9.6 g of oxygen?

$2\text{KClO}_3 \rightarrow 2\text{KCl} + 3\text{O}_2$
questions.explanation:
Molar mass O₂ = 32.00 g/mol. Moles O₂ = 9.6 g / 32.00 g/mol = 0.30 mol.

Mole ratio O₂:KClO₃ = 3:2. Moles KClO₃ = 0.30 mol O₂ × (2 mol KClO₃ / 3 mol O₂) = 0.20 mol.

Molar mass KClO₃ = 39.10 + 35.45 + (3 × 16.00) = 39.10 + 35.45 + 48.00 = 122.55 g/mol. Mass KClO₃ = 0.20 mol × 122.55 g/mol = 24.51 g ≈ 24.5 g.

questions.question: Which of the following has the same mass as 3 moles of N₂O?
questions.explanation:
3 moles N₂O have a mass of 3 × 44 = 132 g (using N = 14, O = 16).

4 moles NO = 4 × 30 = 120 g.
Difference = 12 g = exactly 1 mole of carbon (atomic mass 12).

So 4 moles NO + 1 mole C gives the same total mass as 3 moles N₂O.

Other options:
- A: 3 mol NO₂ (138 g) + C (12 g) = 150 g ❌
- B: 2 mol CO₂ (88 g) + 2 mol O₂ (64 g) = 152 g ❌
- D: 1.5 mol CO₂ (66 g) + 0.5 mol S (16 g) = 82 g ❌

questions.question: A ferritin protein shell has a molecular weight of 450,000 g/mol. In a certain tissue sample, the ferritin is found to be 20% iron by mass. Approximately how many iron atoms are stored within one molecule of this ferritin? (Atomic mass of Fe = 56)
questions.explanation:
Mass of iron = 20% of 450,000 = 0.20 × 450,000 = 90,000 g/mol.
Number of Fe atoms = 90,000 / 56 ≈ 1,607 atoms.
This falls within the biologically expected range for ferritin, which can store up to 4,500 iron atoms.

questions.question: Hemoglobin has a molecular mass of 64,500 g/mol and contains 0.347% iron by mass. How many iron atoms are present in one hemoglobin molecule? (Atomic mass Fe = 56)
questions.explanation:
Mass of iron in 1 mole hemoglobin = (0.347/100) × 64,500 = 0.00347 × 64,500 ≈ 223.8 g.

Moles of Fe in 1 mole hemoglobin = 223.8 / 56 ≈ 4.00.

So each hemoglobin molecule contains 4 iron atoms — one in each of its 4 heme groups.

This matches known biochemistry: hemoglobin has 4 subunits, each binding one Fe²⁺.

questions.question: A sample of the male sex hormone testosterone (C₁₉H₂₈O₂) contains 3.50 × 10²¹ atoms of carbon. How many atoms of oxygen does the sample contain?
questions.explanation:
Each molecule of testosterone has 19 carbon atoms and 2 oxygen atoms.

O atoms / C atoms per molecule = 2/19.

Given carbon atoms = 3.50 × 10²¹,

Oxygen atoms = (3.50 × 10²¹) × (2/19)
= (7.00 × 10²¹) / 19
≈ 3.68 × 10²⁰.

This matches option A.

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered advanced stoichiometry and biochemical applications! 🧪🔬
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review mole ratios and mass calculations carefully.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on setting up the correct ratios in multi-step problems.
scoreMessages.0: Please review stoichiometric calculations, mole ratios, and percent composition concepts.
